title: PX4 SITL on macOS M-Series - Bypass Make System
context: Running 'make px4_sitl gz_x500' on macOS M1/M2/M3 fails or Gazebo is unstable (per PX4 docs: 'UNSTABLE')
what_happened: Direct PX4 binary execution works reliably: PX4_SIM_MODEL=gz_x500 ./build/px4_sitl_default/bin/px4. This bypasses the make build system issues.
why: The make system has additional dependencies and environment setup that can fail on macOS ARM. The binary directly starts the SITL with Gazebo.
action:
Use direct binary execution instead of make:
1. cd PX4-Autopilot
2. PX4_SIM_MODEL=gz_x500 ./build/px4_sitl_default/bin/px4
3. Gazebo will auto-start with the x500 model
source: PX4 docs list Gazebo on macOS as UNSTABLE - but this workaround works
